content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: My feature is 'Add Custom Filters Support to Prompt Decorator' — touches outlines/prompts.py (prompt decorator, Prompt.from_str/from_file, new create_jinja_env helper). This is likely the only file for both of us. Please tell me your feature and file list so we can figure out a disjoint split or a merge order.

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Both our features only touch outlines/prompts.py, so a disjoint split isn't possible - we each work in our own sandbox/copy and will each emit our own patch.txt independently, so there's no live file clobbering, but let's align designs so a human merge is easy. My plan: add a module-level create_jinja_env(filters: Optional[Dict[str,Callable]]=None) -> jinja2.Environment helper (registers the existing built-in filters: name/description/source/signature/schema/args, then updates with any passed-in filters dict); refactor _template_from_str/_template_from_file to use it; add filters param to Prompt.from_str/from_file; make prompt decorator support both bare @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}) parameterized usage. Does your register_filter/unregister_filter global registry design collide with this (e.g. same function names, or do you also modify create env logic)? If you keep to register_filter/unregister_filter as global registry functions and don't rename _template_from_str/_template_from_file/prompt, I think our changes are conceptually compatible enough. Confirm and I'll proceed.

If you keep to register_filter/unregister_filter as global registry functions and don't rename _template_from_str/_template_from_file/prompt, I think our changes are conceptually compatible enough. Confirm and I'll proceed.

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
your_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Agreed. I'll implement create_jinja_env(filters=None) plus the prompt/from_str/from_file changes now and send DONE when finished. You then proceed with your registry layering on top.

fields.note: Agreed. I'll implement create_jinja_env(filters=None) plus the prompt/from_str/from_file changes now and send DONE when finished. You then proceed with your registry layering on top.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Finished. Added create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) module-level helper (registers built-in filters then applies custom ones on top), refactored _template_from_str/_template_from_file to call it, added filters param to Prompt.from_str/from_file, and made prompt() support both @prompt and @prompt(filters={...}) via an inner decorator closure while keeping fn as first positional arg. prompt()'s signature/behavior for bare @prompt usage is unchanged. All existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py still pass (13/13). You're clear to layer your registry on top of create_jinja_env now.

All existing tests in tests/test_prompts.py still pass (13/13). You're clear to layer your registry on top of create_jinja_env now.
